doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_powerof2_raw (mpfr/src/powerof2.c L30-L38). A thin wrapper around mpfr_powerof2_raw2: extracts the mantissa from an MPFR value and asks whether it is exactly a power of two (i.e. only the MSB at position prec-1 is set). The C function reads MPFR_MANT(x) without asserting MPFR_IS_PURE_FP (the assertion is documented but disabled on L36); for singular kinds the mantissa storage is undefined. The TS port folds the kind check into the wrapper: NaN/Inf/Zero return false (no MSB-aligned mantissa to inspect; mant === 0n in the schema), normal kinds delegate to raw2. This matches the C contract at the I/O level: in C, calling on a zero-mantissa singular value happens to return false (xp[xn-1] != MPFR_LIMB_HIGHBIT), and the TS port returns the same false explicitly. Pure predicate; no exceptions, no allocations.
divergence_from_c: C takes mpfr_srcptr (a pointer); TS takes an MPFR value by value. C returns int (0/1); TS returns boolean. C reads MPFR_MANT(x) regardless of kind (the C function comment at L33-L36 explicitly notes that callers may pass 'wrong numbers'); the TS port reads x.mant only for normal kinds and returns false for singular kinds (the schema's mant === 0n for singular kinds would yield the same false anyway). No state, no thrown exceptions.
refs: mpfr/src/powerof2.c L30-L38 — the C reference body for mpfr_powerof2_raw.
src/internal/mpfr/powerof2_raw.ts — production port for this function.
src/core.ts L93-L135 — locked MPFR value model; mant === 0n for singular kinds.
CLAUDE.md Law 3 — faithful substrate, idiomatic surface.
